raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "California Democrats are seeking to weaken the criminal justice system and release more criminals into communities.",
    "A Democratic state senator proposed allowing criminals convicted of serious felonies to serve on a jury immediately upon release from jail or prison.",
    "Under current California law, a person convicted of a felony cannot serve on a jury.",
    "Senate Bill 310 eliminates the process that restores civil and political rights for rehabilitated criminals and makes it automatic without investigation.",
    "Convicted felons would no longer have to demonstrate rehabilitation, remorse, or make amends to serve on a jury.",
    "Felons convicted of violent or serious property and drug crimes leave lasting impacts on their victims.",
    "Senate Republicans believe that assisting formerly convicted individuals in re-entering society should focus on rehabilitation, job training, and drug treatment."
  ]
}
```
